description: Configuration for the nightly Dream consolidation. Edit paths/thresholds here; harvest.py, ledger.py, run-dream.ps1 and the dream prompt all read this file. Copy this file to config.json and fill in your own values.

identity._comment: Used to attribute git commits to you. List every email/name you author commits under.

model_policy.note: Configurable. Set 'allowed'/'default' to whichever models your Copilot CLI supports; run-dream.ps1's -Model ValidateSet must match. By default both run at 1M context (long_context) with max reasoning.

window.note: Harvest window = now - min(hours since last successful run, max_hours), floored at default_hours on first run.

map_reduce.note: The nightly Dream runs as map-reduce for quality (quality degrades long before a single agent fills a 1M context). shard.py splits the harvest into <=max_shards balanced shards (~target_tokens each, threads never split); one parallel classifier sub-agent per shard (MAP); reduce.py merges+dedups (REDUCE); one parallel sub-agent per target skill applies edits (APPLY), capped at apply_max_parallel. The orchestrator stays lean - it holds only compact JSON, never raw session or skill bodies - so every sub-agent works in a fresh high-quality context and no compaction is needed. Set enabled=false to fall back to a single-agent pass on light days.

git_commits._comment: EDIT THIS: point roots_glob at the parent folders that contain your git repos. Each glob is expanded, and any matched subfolder containing a .git dir is scanned for your commits. Use roots_exclude to skip repos the globs would otherwise match (each entry is matched as a glob AND as a case-insensitive substring against the repo's full path) - e.g. a personal side-project.

domain.off_domain_note: Off-domain personal projects and machine-maintenance chatter are DROPPED from your reference skills. Keep an item only if it reveals a durable, reusable dev-workflow lesson.

targets._comment: List YOUR durable reference skills here; OR leave this empty to let the Dream auto-seed a single 'knowledge-base' skill and propose dedicated ones over time (which you approve). Example entries you might add: 'service-architecture': '~/.copilot/skills/service-architecture/SKILL.md', 'deployment-runbook': '~/.copilot/skills/deployment-runbook/SKILL.md', 'telemetry-queries': '~/.copilot/skills/telemetry-queries/SKILL.md', 'team-conventions': '~/.copilot/skills/team-conventions/SKILL.md'.

agent_memory.note: Optional. Repo-specific coding patterns go to per-repo .github/agent-history/*.md via a per-repo history protocol, NOT to personal skills. Remove this block if you do not use one.

targets.durable_filter_note: Optional. A skill describing your durable-vs-transient KEEP/DROP filter. Leave the path pointing at a non-existent skill (or remove) if you do not have one.

read_only_context.note: EDIT THIS (optional): read-only reference sources the Dream CONSULTS but NEVER edits. Point these at YOUR repos' own agent guidance and in-repo skills. During MAP, a classifier sub-agent reads the file(s) matching its shard's repo so extracted knowledge aligns with that repo's conventions, and repo-owned knowledge is deferred to the repo (its agent-history and in-repo skills) instead of being duplicated into your personal skills. Non-existent paths are skipped. Leave the arrays empty if you do not want any repo context.

seed.note: Cold-start behavior. The Dream always ensures the index (dream) and active-work (dream-active-work) skills exist, creating them from a minimal template if missing. If targets.long_term_skills is EMPTY, it also seeds the single general_skill below ('knowledge-base') and routes durable facts there until enough recurring topics emerge to PROPOSE dedicated skills (which you approve). No-op once you have any long-term skill.

general_skill.name: knowledge-base
general_skill.description: Personal engineering knowledge base - durable facts, conventions, runbooks, and resource links captured by the nightly Dream. Auto-seeded; split into dedicated skills as topics grow.

thresholds.note: SHORT items with hit_count>=promote_hit_count over >=promote_distinct_days distinct days are promotion candidates. Active SHORT items untouched for decay_days are archived. Only HIGH-confidence LONG items auto-apply; others go to review-queue. Items with importance < importance_keep_floor are dropped unless part of an active thread.
